filesystem.description: Reference MCP server letting agents read/write/list files under an allow-listed directory. Run with least-privilege paths and read-only mode for sensitive data; ideal when you want to scope a project folder as an agent workspace.

git.description: Reference MCP server exposing common git operations (status, log, diff, blame) to an agent. Great starting point for read-only code reviewers; grant write access carefully.

github.description: GitHub’s official MCP server: read/write repos, manage issues/PRs, and search code. Use scoped PATs or a GitHub App with audit logging for production.

gitlab.description: GitLab: official MCP server from Model Context Protocol—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

postgres.description: Reference MCP server for read-only Postgres access and schema introspection—ideal for data-analysis agents. Lock down with a read-only role and schema allow-list.

sqlite.description: SQLite: official MCP server from Model Context Protocol—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

memory.description: Memory: official MCP server from Model Context Protocol—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

fetch.description: Reference MCP server that fetches any HTTP URL and converts it to Markdown—often the minimal “browsing” capability for agents. Add a domain allow-list and rate limits.

puppeteer.description: Puppeteer: official MCP server from Model Context Protocol—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

playwright-mcp.description: Microsoft’s Playwright MCP server: cross-browser automation with broader coverage than Puppeteer—fits scrapers and UI-testing agents that need headless Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit.

time.description: Time: official MCP server from Model Context Protocol—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

sequential-thinking.description: Sequential Thinking: official MCP server from Model Context Protocol—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

brave-search.description: Brave Search: official MCP server from Model Context Protocol—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

google-drive.description: Google Drive: official MCP server from Model Context Protocol—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

google-maps.description: Google Maps: official MCP server from Model Context Protocol—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

slack.description: Reference MCP server that reads/writes Slack messages and searches history—turns Slack into an agent’s comms layer. Use a bot token with minimal scopes.

sentry.description: Sentry: official MCP server from Model Context Protocol—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

cloudflare-mcp.description: Cloudflare’s official MCP suite covering Workers, KV, D1, R2, Pages, and DNS. Supports Remote MCP over HTTP, so you can plug it into Claude or Cursor without a local binary.

stripe-mcp.description: Stripe’s official MCP server exposing accounts, orders, refunds, and products as tools—handy for commerce agents. Restrict to test / restricted keys in production.

linear-mcp.description: Linear’s official MCP server: create and query Issues, Projects, and Cycles—a lightweight task pipeline for engineering/product agents. Auth via personal API key or OAuth.

notion-mcp.description: Notion’s official MCP server: read/write pages and database items—perfect for wiring requirement docs, weekly reports, and knowledge bases into agents. Workspace-level auth applies.

atlassian-mcp.description: Atlassian Remote MCP: official MCP server from Atlassian—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

supabase-mcp.description: Supabase’s official MCP server covering database, Storage, and Auth operations—great for wiring a Supabase project into AI IDEs and ops agents.

neon-mcp.description: Neon: official MCP server from Neon—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

mongodb-mcp.description: MongoDB: official MCP server from MongoDB—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

exa-mcp.description: Exa: official MCP server from Exa—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

tavily-mcp.description: Tavily MCP server exposes the agent-focused search API as MCP tools—an easy way to give any MCP client a “can search the web” capability with API key control.

firecrawl-mcp.description: Firecrawl: official MCP server from Mendable / Firecrawl—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

e2b-mcp.description: E2B Code Interpreter: official MCP server from E2B—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.

apify-mcp.description: Apify Actors: official MCP server from Apify—confirm version, auth scopes, and transport with the upstream docs before production use.
